mnemonics: ANOVA VIBES: Variance In Between Equals Significance (to remember that ANOVA compares variance between groups to variance within groups to determine if means are significantly different)
keyPoints: ANOVA (Analysis of Variance) is used to compare the means of three or more groups to determine if at least one is statistically different.
The F-statistic is the key test statistic in ANOVA, calculated as the ratio of between-group variance to within-group variance.
The experimental design (e.g., one-way, factorial) determines the type of ANOVA used and how variability is partitioned.
commonMistakes: Assuming ANOVA tells you which specific groups are different (it only indicates a difference exists; post-hoc tests are needed for specific comparisons).
Applying ANOVA to data that violates its assumptions, such as lack of normality or homogeneity of variances, without using a robust alternative or transformation.
